description: Test fixtures manifest for metadata_audio library. The project uses synthetic data generation for test fixtures rather than storing binary audio files.

synthetic_fixtures.description: FLAC file with StreamInfo, Vorbis comments, and picture metadata blocks
characteristics: StreamInfo block with 44.1kHz sample rate, 2 channels, 16-bit
Picture block with JPEG image data
supported_variations: Custom stream info (sample rate, channels, bit depth)
Picture data with custom mime types

characteristics: ID3v1 tag format with fixed 128-byte structure
ID3v2.2 frame format with 3-character frame identifiers
ID3v2.3 frame format with 4-character frame identifiers
ID3v2.4 frame format with 4-character frame identifiers
MPEG frame headers with various configurations
MP3 audio data with ID3 tags
supported_variations: Various bitrate and sample rate combinations

synthetic_fixtures.description: WAV file format with RIFF headers and LIST-INFO chunks

characteristics: DSF file format with DSD audio

synthetic_fixtures.description: Mock tokenizer for testing binary data parsing without actual files

testing_approach: All tests use synthetic data generation rather than storing binary fixture files. This approach:
benefits: Avoids copyright/licensing issues with real audio files
Allows testing of edge cases and malformed data
adding_new_fixtures: When adding new test fixtures, follow the synthetic generation pattern: 1) Create builder functions that construct byte-accurate audio format representations, 2) Document the builder functions and supported variations, 3) Update this MANIFEST.json with generator_location and characteristics, 4) Avoid storing binary files in test/fixtures/ directories
